_comment: 30 marathon-relevant bodyweight lower-body / glute exercises. Each gets one muscle-activation diagram. Codes match COROS exercise_catalog.md.

exercises.posture: Athletic person at the bottom of a bodyweight squat: feet shoulder-width apart and flat on the ground, knees tracking over toes, thighs roughly parallel to the floor (deep squat depth), hips pushed back, torso leaning slightly forward with neutral spine and chest up, both arms extended forward at shoulder height for balance, wearing plain black athletic shorts and a fitted gray tank top
exercises.primary_muscles_en: quadriceps (front of thighs) and gluteus maximus
exercises.secondary_muscles_en: hamstring group and erector spinae along the lower back
labels_zh: the hamstring and erector spinae area

exercises.posture: Athletic person at the moment of takeoff in a jumping squat: feet shoulder-width apart leaving the ground, ankles fully extended (plantarflexed), knees and hips fully extended driving upward, arms swinging forward and up for momentum, torso upright, body in vertical alignment, plain black athletic shorts and gray tank top
exercises.primary_muscles_en: quadriceps and gluteus maximus and calf muscles (gastrocnemius)
exercises.secondary_muscles_en: hamstring group and erector spinae

exercises.posture: Athletic person at the bottom of a single-leg squat (pistol squat): right leg supporting, right knee deeply bent so right thigh is parallel to the floor or slightly below, right foot flat on the ground; left leg fully extended straight forward off the ground, parallel to the floor; both arms extended forward at shoulder height for counterbalance; torso leaning forward with neutral spine; plain black athletic shorts and gray tank top
exercises.primary_muscles_en: quadriceps and gluteus maximus of the supporting leg
exercises.secondary_muscles_en: hamstring group and gluteus medius of the supporting hip and core stabilizers
labels_zh: the quadriceps of the supporting leg
the gluteus maximus of the supporting leg
the gluteus medius and core area

exercises.posture: Athletic person at the bottom of a Bulgarian split squat: front leg (right) supporting with foot flat on the ground and knee bent at 90 degrees with thigh parallel to the floor, knee tracking over toes; rear leg (left) extended back with the top of the rear foot resting on a knee-height platform behind, rear knee hovering just above the ground, rear hip and knee both bent; torso upright with neutral spine, hands on hips, plain black athletic shorts and gray tank top
exercises.primary_muscles_en: quadriceps and gluteus maximus of the front leg
exercises.secondary_muscles_en: hamstring group of the front leg and hip flexors of the rear leg
labels_zh: the quadriceps of the front leg
the gluteus maximus of the front leg
the hamstring and hip flexor area

exercises.posture: Athletic person at the bottom of a lateral squat: feet wider than shoulder-width apart, weight shifted entirely onto the right leg with right knee bent at roughly 90 degrees and right thigh parallel to the floor, right foot flat on the ground; left leg fully extended straight to the side with foot flat on the ground and toe pointing forward; hips pushed back, torso upright with slight forward lean, hands clasped at chest height for balance, plain black athletic shorts and gray tank top
exercises.primary_muscles_en: quadriceps and gluteus maximus of the bent (right) leg and the adductor muscles of the extended (left) inner thigh
exercises.secondary_muscles_en: gluteus medius of the bent leg and hamstring group
labels_zh: the quadriceps of the bent leg
the glutes of the bent leg
the adductor muscles of the extended inner thigh

exercises.posture: Athletic person at the bottom of a curtsey squat: right leg standing with right foot flat and right knee bent to roughly 90 degrees; left leg crossed behind and to the right of the right leg with left foot on the ball of the foot and left knee bent toward the floor without touching it (like a deep curtsey); hips and shoulders facing forward, torso upright with neutral spine, hands clasped at chest height, plain black athletic shorts and gray tank top
exercises.primary_muscles_en: gluteus maximus and gluteus medius of the front (right) leg, plus quadriceps
exercises.secondary_muscles_en: adductor muscles and hamstring group
labels_zh: the glute muscles of the front leg

exercises.posture: Athletic person in a wall-sit isometric hold: back, shoulders, and head pressed flat against a vertical wall; feet shoulder-width apart and flat on the ground, positioned about a thigh-length out from the wall; knees bent at exactly 90 degrees with thighs parallel to the floor; shins vertical; arms hanging relaxed at sides or crossed at chest, plain black athletic shorts and gray tank top
exercises.primary_muscles_en: quadriceps (front of thighs) holding the isometric position
exercises.secondary_muscles_en: gluteus maximus and core muscles

exercises.posture: Athletic person at the top of a single-leg deadlift to knee drive: right leg standing with a slight bend at the knee; left knee driven up high in front of the body to hip height with thigh parallel to the floor and shin perpendicular (90 degree knee bend); torso upright with neutral spine; arms swinging in opposition (right arm forward, left arm back) like a running stride; plain black athletic shorts and gray tank top
exercises.primary_muscles_en: gluteus maximus and hamstring of the supporting (right) leg, hip flexors (psoas and rectus femoris) of the lifted (left) leg
exercises.secondary_muscles_en: core muscles (transverse abdominis) and erector spinae
labels_zh: the glute and hamstring of the supporting leg
the hip flexors of the lifted leg
the core and erector spinae area

exercises.posture: Athletic person at the bottom of a jumping lunge just before takeoff: front leg (right) bent at 90 degrees with thigh parallel to the floor, foot flat; rear leg (left) bent at 90 degrees with knee just above the ground, ball of the rear foot on the ground; torso upright with neutral spine, arms swinging in running-style opposition (left arm forward, right arm back); plain black athletic shorts and gray tank top
exercises.primary_muscles_en: quadriceps and gluteus maximus of the front leg, calf muscles for jump propulsion
exercises.secondary_muscles_en: hamstring group and hip flexors
labels_zh: the quadriceps of the front leg
小腿三头肌 / 腘绳肌

exercises.posture: Athletic person mid-stride during a walking lunge: front leg (right) just landed with foot flat and knee bent at 90 degrees, thigh parallel to the floor; rear leg (left) extended back with knee bent and hovering just above the ground, ball of the rear foot on the ground; torso upright with neutral spine, hands on hips, plain black athletic shorts and gray tank top
exercises.primary_muscles_en: quadriceps and gluteus maximus of the front leg
exercises.secondary_muscles_en: hamstring group of the front leg and hip flexors of the rear leg
labels_zh: the quadriceps of the front leg
the hamstring and hip flexor area

exercises.posture: Athletic person at the bottom of a reverse lunge: front leg (right) standing in place with foot flat and knee bent at 90 degrees, thigh parallel to the floor; rear leg (left) stepped back with knee bent and hovering just above the ground, ball of the rear foot on the ground; torso upright with neutral spine, hands on hips, plain black athletic shorts and gray tank top
exercises.primary_muscles_en: gluteus maximus and hamstring group of the front leg, plus quadriceps
exercises.secondary_muscles_en: hip flexors of the rear leg and core muscles
labels_zh: the gluteus maximus of the front leg
the hip flexors of the rear leg

exercises.posture: Athletic person at the top of a lunge with knee lift: standing on the right leg with right knee almost fully extended (slight bend); left knee driven up high in front of the body to hip height, thigh parallel to floor, shin perpendicular; torso upright; arms in running-style opposition; plain black athletic shorts and gray tank top
exercises.primary_muscles_en: gluteus maximus and quadriceps of the supporting (right) leg, plus hip flexors (psoas) of the lifted (left) leg
exercises.secondary_muscles_en: core muscles and gluteus medius
labels_zh: the glute and quad of the supporting leg
the hip flexors of the lifted leg
the core and gluteus medius area

exercises.posture: Athletic person at the top of a glute bridge: lying supine on the floor with upper back, head, neck, and arms flat on the ground; both feet flat on the ground hip-width apart with both knees bent at 90 degrees; hips lifted upward so that the body forms a straight diagonal line from the knees through the hips to the shoulders; arms resting at the sides with palms down; plain black athletic shorts and gray tank top
exercises.primary_muscles_en: gluteus maximus (the main hip extensors) and hamstring group
exercises.secondary_muscles_en: core muscles (transverse abdominis) and erector spinae
labels_zh: the core and erector spinae area

exercises.view_angle: low-angle side view from ground level looking horizontally across the body
exercises.posture: Pilates-style demonstration: athletic person performing a single-leg glute bridge HOLD, photographed from a low ground-level camera looking horizontally across the body. The person is lying supine on the floor with shoulders, head, and arms flat on the ground. The right foot is on the ground with right knee bent 90 degrees, supporting the body. The hips are lifted into a bridge. The KEY visual: the LEFT LEG is FULLY STRAIGHTENED and held PARALLEL TO THE FLOOR like a horizontal pointer extending out from the body — left leg, hips, and right shoulder all align in one HORIZONTAL line that is parallel to the floor. The left toe points away from the body horizontally. Imagine an arrow lying flat on the floor, with the body forming the back of the arrow and the left leg pointing as the arrow's shaft. Plain black athletic shorts and gray tank top. NEGATIVE: the left leg is NOT pointing toward the ceiling, NOT angled upward at all, NOT bent at the knee — it is COMPLETELY HORIZONTAL, parallel to the ground.
exercises.primary_muscles_en: gluteus maximus and hamstring group of the supporting (right) leg
exercises.secondary_muscles_en: core muscles and erector spinae and gluteus medius for hip stability
labels_zh: the gluteus maximus of the supporting leg
the hamstring group of the supporting leg
the core and erector spinae area

exercises.posture: Athletic person holding a glute bridge in isometric position: lying supine with upper back, head, neck, and arms flat on the ground; both feet flat on the ground hip-width apart with both knees bent at 90 degrees; hips lifted maximally so the body forms a strong straight line from knees to shoulders, glutes fully squeezed; arms flat at sides with palms down; plain black athletic shorts and gray tank top
exercises.primary_muscles_en: gluteus maximus (under maximal isometric contraction)
exercises.secondary_muscles_en: hamstring group and core muscles and erector spinae
labels_zh: the core and erector spinae area

exercises.posture: Athletic person at the top of a donkey kick: in a quadruped position with hands flat on the ground directly under shoulders and right knee on the ground directly under hip; left leg lifted with knee still bent at 90 degrees and the sole of the left foot pushing toward the ceiling, thigh parallel to the ground or slightly higher; spine neutral, hips and shoulders level (no rotation); plain black athletic shorts and gray tank top
exercises.primary_muscles_en: gluteus maximus of the kicking (left) leg
exercises.secondary_muscles_en: hamstring group of the kicking leg and core muscles for stability
labels_zh: the gluteus maximus of the kicking leg

exercises.posture: Athletic person doing a clamshell exercise: lying on the right side on the ground; both knees bent to about 45 degrees with feet stacked and touching each other; hips stacked vertically; the top knee (left) is rotated open and clearly lifted away from the bottom knee while the feet remain in contact, like a clamshell opening; right arm tucked under the head, left hand resting on the hip; spine neutral; plain black athletic shorts and gray tank top
exercises.primary_muscles_en: gluteus medius of the top hip
exercises.secondary_muscles_en: gluteus maximus (upper fibers) and deep external hip rotators
labels_zh: the gluteus medius of the top hip

exercises.posture: Athletic person performing a high-knee drill at peak knee lift: right leg supporting with foot in mid-stride contact with the ground; left knee driven up explosively to hip height or higher with thigh at or above parallel to the floor and shin perpendicular; arms in running-style opposition (right arm forward bent at 90 degrees, left arm back bent at 90 degrees); torso upright with slight forward lean; plain black athletic shorts and gray tank top
exercises.primary_muscles_en: hip flexors (psoas major and rectus femoris) of the lifted leg, and calf muscles of the supporting leg
exercises.secondary_muscles_en: gluteus maximus of the supporting leg and core muscles
labels_zh: the hip flexors of the lifted leg
the calf muscles of the supporting leg

exercises.posture: Athletic person performing a hands-on-wall high-knee drill: leaning forward at about 45 degrees with both hands flat against a vertical wall at shoulder height, arms straight; right leg supporting on the ball of the foot; left knee driven up high in front of the body to hip height with thigh parallel to the floor and shin perpendicular (90 degree knee angle); torso forms one straight line from heels through hips to head; plain black athletic shorts and gray tank top
exercises.primary_muscles_en: hip flexors (psoas) of the lifted leg and calf muscles of the supporting leg
exercises.secondary_muscles_en: gluteus maximus of the supporting leg and core muscles
labels_zh: the hip flexors of the lifted leg
the calf muscles of the supporting leg

exercises.posture: Athletic person at the top phase of an A-skip drill: right foot on the ball of the foot pushing off the ground; left thigh driven up to parallel with the floor (knee at hip height), left knee bent at 90 degrees with shin perpendicular and toes pulled up (dorsiflexed); arms in opposition like running; torso upright with slight forward lean; this is the classic A-skip drill posture used by sprinters; plain black athletic shorts and gray tank top
exercises.primary_muscles_en: hip flexors of the lifted leg and calf muscles of the pushing leg
exercises.secondary_muscles_en: gluteus maximus and tibialis anterior (front of shin)
labels_zh: the hip flexors of the lifted leg
the calf muscles of the pushing leg
the tibialis anterior and glute area

exercises.posture: Athletic person at the SIGNATURE LEG-EXTENSION + PAW-DOWN phase of a B-skip drill: right foot on the ball of the foot supporting on the ground. The LEFT LEG is the active drill leg and is in the DEFINING B-SKIP shape: left thigh is HELD AT PARALLEL to the floor (knee at hip height) AND the LEFT LOWER LEG (shin) IS FULLY EXTENDED FORWARD with the knee almost straightened — the foot is pointed forward and slightly down as if pawing or kicking forward, ready to whip back toward the ground. So the entire left leg is extended out in front, thigh horizontal and shin extending out from there, almost like a kicking motion. Arms in running-style opposition. Torso upright with slight forward lean. Plain black athletic shorts and gray tank top. CRITICAL: the LEFT LEG IS NOT BENT 90° (that would be A-skip) — the lower leg is EXTENDED FORWARD AT THE KNEE in a forward-paw position. This is the visual difference from A-skip.
exercises.primary_muscles_en: hamstring group of the extending leg (eccentric loading) and hip flexors and calf muscles
exercises.secondary_muscles_en: gluteus maximus and core muscles
labels_zh: the hamstrings of the extending leg

exercises.view_angle: side view, facing right, freeze-frame photographic style
exercises.posture: Photographic freeze-frame of a STRAIGHT-LEG bounding drill, side view. The athlete is suspended IN MID-AIR — about 15-25 cm of CLEAR EMPTY FLOOR SPACE is visible directly below both feet (a small shadow on the ground far below the body confirms airborne position). BOTH KNEES ARE LOCKED COMPLETELY STRAIGHT — zero knee bend, both legs RIGID like sticks. The FRONT (left) leg is locked-out and angled forward at about 30 degrees from vertical with the foot DORSIFLEXED (toes pulled UP). The REAR (right) leg is locked-out and angled back at about 30 degrees from vertical with the foot PLANTARFLEXED (toes pointed DOWN). Together the two stiff-straight legs form a wide scissor pattern in the air. Arms swinging in vigorous running-style opposition. Body traveling forward (rightward). Plain black athletic shorts and gray tank top. NEGATIVE: this is NOT running with bent knees — both knees are FULLY STRAIGHT throughout the movement; both feet are OFF THE GROUND.
exercises.primary_muscles_en: calf muscles (gastrocnemius and soleus) for elastic recoil and hamstring group
exercises.secondary_muscles_en: gluteus maximus and quadriceps
labels_zh: the calf muscles for elastic recoil

exercises.posture: Athletic person at the peak of a single-leg high-knee jump: right leg fully extended pushing off the ground (toes about to leave or just left the ground); left thigh driven up high to hip height or above with knee bent at 90 degrees; arms in running-style opposition; torso upright; explosive single-leg jump posture; plain black athletic shorts and gray tank top
exercises.primary_muscles_en: calf muscles and quadriceps and gluteus maximus of the jumping (right) leg
exercises.secondary_muscles_en: hip flexors of the lifted leg and core muscles
labels_zh: the calf and quadriceps of the jumping leg
the hip flexors and core area

exercises.view_angle: side view, facing right, freeze-frame photographic style
exercises.posture: Photographic freeze-frame showing an athlete suspended IN MID-AIR during a forward single-leg hop. EVIDENCE OF AIRBORNE POSITION: about 15-25 cm of CLEAR EMPTY FLOOR SPACE is visible directly below the right foot (the hopping foot) — the floor/ground is rendered with a small visible shadow far below the body. The LEFT LEG is HELD UP throughout the entire hop with the left knee bent at 90 degrees, left thigh horizontal — the LEFT FOOT NEVER TOUCHES THE GROUND in this drill. Only the RIGHT LEG provides propulsion; in this freeze-frame the right leg is mid-flight with knee slightly bent. Arms swing for momentum. Torso slightly forward-leaning showing forward motion. Plain black athletic shorts and gray tank top. NEGATIVE: NOT a regular running stride with both feet alternating on the ground — both feet are CURRENTLY OFF THE GROUND, the body is suspended in the air, and the LEFT foot has been held up the entire time.
exercises.primary_muscles_en: calf muscles and quadriceps and gluteus maximus of the hopping leg
exercises.secondary_muscles_en: hamstring group and core muscles for stabilization
labels_zh: the calf and quadriceps of the hopping leg

exercises.posture: Athletic person at the top of a box step-up: right foot fully planted on top of a knee-height box or platform with right knee fully extended supporting the body weight; left leg lifted with thigh at hip height (parallel to floor) and knee bent at 90 degrees; torso upright with neutral spine; arms in running-style opposition; plain black athletic shorts and gray tank top
exercises.primary_muscles_en: quadriceps and gluteus maximus of the standing (right) leg
exercises.secondary_muscles_en: hamstring group of the standing leg and hip flexors of the lifted leg
labels_zh: the quadriceps of the standing leg
the hamstring and hip flexor area

exercises.posture: Athletic person at the top of a standing side leg raise: right leg supporting with foot flat on the ground and knee almost fully extended (slight bend); left leg fully extended straight out to the side at about 30-45 degrees away from the body, foot flexed (toes pointing forward); torso upright with neutral spine, hands on hips for support; plain black athletic shorts and gray tank top
exercises.primary_muscles_en: gluteus medius of the lifted (left) hip
exercises.secondary_muscles_en: gluteus maximus and core muscles for stability
labels_zh: the gluteus medius of the lifted hip

exercises.posture: Athletic person at the front phase of a forward leg swing (dynamic stretch / activation): right leg supporting with foot flat on the ground; left leg swung forward and up to hip height with knee almost fully extended; torso upright, right hand placed on a wall or support to the right for balance; this is a controlled dynamic mobility movement; plain black athletic shorts and gray tank top
exercises.primary_muscles_en: hip flexors (psoas) of the swinging leg, with eccentric load on the hamstring group
exercises.secondary_muscles_en: gluteus maximus of the supporting leg and core muscles
labels_zh: the hip flexors of the swinging leg

exercises.view_angle: side view from ground level, the lower legs and feet fill the lower half of the frame
exercises.posture: Athletic person standing on TIPTOES like a ballet dancer en pointe — only the BALLS OF THE TOES touch the ground. The HEELS are lifted HIGH IN THE AIR with a large visible gap between heels and floor (about 8-10 cm of empty space). The ankle joints are in EXTREME plantarflexion, the back of the ankles deeply pinched. Both legs are straight and parallel, hip-width apart. The figure looks taller than normal because of the heel lift. Torso upright, arms relaxed at the sides. Plain black athletic shorts and gray tank top. EMPHASIS: this person is balanced on TIPTOES like a ballerina — the heels are dramatically OFF THE FLOOR. The figure is taller than a normal standing pose because of the elevated stance.
exercises.primary_muscles_en: calf muscles (gastrocnemius and soleus)
exercises.secondary_muscles_en: tibialis posterior and small intrinsic foot muscles

exercises.posture: Athletic person mid-descent in an inverse Nordic curl: kneeling on the ground with both knees on a soft pad, shins flat on the ground, toes pointed, and ankles flat; the body is leaning backward as a single straight rigid line from knees to head, currently at about 45 degrees from vertical (descending under control); arms crossed over the chest or extended forward for counterbalance; the quadriceps work eccentrically to control the descent; plain black athletic shorts and gray tank top
exercises.primary_muscles_en: quadriceps (under heavy eccentric load)
exercises.secondary_muscles_en: hip flexors and core muscles for spinal stability

exercises.posture: Athletic person at the top of a standing hamstring curl: right leg supporting with foot flat on the ground and knee almost fully extended (slight bend); left leg lifted behind the body with knee bent so that the left foot heel is brought toward the left buttock (knee fully flexed), the left thigh remains roughly vertical (perpendicular to the floor); torso upright with neutral spine, right hand placed on a wall in front for balance; plain black athletic shorts and gray tank top
exercises.primary_muscles_en: hamstring group of the curling (left) leg
exercises.secondary_muscles_en: gluteus maximus of the curling leg and core muscles
labels_zh: the hamstring group of the curling leg

exercises.view_angle: three-quarter view from above and side
exercises.posture: Athletic person performing an alternating single-leg supine hip lift: lying supine on the floor with upper back, head, neck, shoulders, and arms flat on the ground, arms relaxed at sides palms down; both knees bent at 90 degrees with feet flat on the ground hip-width apart at the start; right leg lifted off the ground with the right knee still bent at 90 degrees (right foot off the ground); left foot remains flat on the ground (this is the supporting leg); hips lifted upward so that left thigh, hips, and shoulders form one straight diagonal line (about 30 degrees from the floor); plain black athletic shorts and gray tank top
exercises.primary_muscles_en: gluteus maximus and hamstring group of the supporting (left) leg
exercises.secondary_muscles_en: core muscles and gluteus medius of the supporting hip
labels_zh: the gluteus maximus of the supporting leg
the core and gluteus medius area

exercises.name_en: single-leg supine hip and knee lift
exercises.posture: Athletic person at the top of a single-leg supine bridge with the LIFTED LEG TUCKED TO CHEST: lying SUPINE on the floor with upper back, head, neck, and arms flat on the ground; LEFT foot flat on the ground with left knee bent at 90 degrees — this is the SUPPORTING leg. The RIGHT leg is the LIFTED leg and is in DEEP HIP+KNEE FLEXION — the right knee is pulled TOWARD THE CHEST so that the right thigh is roughly VERTICAL (perpendicular to the floor) and the right shin is angled toward the head, with the right knee positioned ABOVE the right hip; the right foot hovers in the air near the head. Hips are lifted off the ground into a bridge so the supporting (left) thigh, hips, and shoulders form a diagonal straight line. Arms flat at the sides palms down. Plain black athletic shorts and gray tank top. CRITICAL: the right knee is pulled high to the chest, NOT extended out forward, NOT lying flat.
exercises.primary_muscles_en: gluteus maximus and hamstring group of the supporting (left) leg, plus hip flexors of the lifted (right) leg
exercises.secondary_muscles_en: core muscles and erector spinae
labels_zh: the glute and hamstring of the supporting leg
the hip flexors of the lifted leg
the core and erector spinae area

exercises.view_angle: side view from a low angle, facing right
exercises.posture: Athletic person mid-rep of a single-leg bridge (bottom of motion, hips just barely lifted off the ground): lying supine with upper back, head, and arms flat on the ground; left foot flat on the ground with left knee bent at 90 degrees (supporting leg); right leg fully extended straight forward parallel to the floor; hips just starting to lift so that hips are about 5 cm off the ground (low, controlled position, not full extension); arms flat at sides palms down; plain black athletic shorts and gray tank top
exercises.primary_muscles_en: gluteus maximus and hamstring group of the supporting (left) leg
exercises.secondary_muscles_en: core muscles and erector spinae
labels_zh: the core and erector spinae area

exercises.posture: Athletic person at the explosive top phase of a lunge with high knee drive (just having stood up from a lunge): right leg fully extended supporting; left knee driven up high to hip height with thigh parallel to the floor and knee bent at 90 degrees with shin perpendicular; arms in running-style opposition (right arm forward, left arm back); torso upright with slight forward lean; plain black athletic shorts and gray tank top
exercises.primary_muscles_en: gluteus maximus and quadriceps of the supporting (right) leg, plus hip flexors of the lifted (left) leg
exercises.secondary_muscles_en: core muscles and gluteus medius for stability
labels_zh: the glute and quadriceps of the supporting leg
the hip flexors of the lifted leg

exercises.posture: Athletic person mid-air during a jumping forward lunge: airborne, both legs extended forward and back like in mid-stride; the front (right) leg is preparing to land in a deep lunge with knee starting to bend; the rear (left) leg is extended back with knee slightly bent; arms in running-style opposition; torso upright; explosive forward jumping motion; plain black athletic shorts and gray tank top
exercises.primary_muscles_en: quadriceps and gluteus maximus and calf muscles for jump propulsion
exercises.secondary_muscles_en: hamstring group and hip flexors and core muscles

exercises.view_angle: front view, facing the viewer, freeze-frame photographic style
exercises.posture: Photographic freeze-frame, front view, of a lateral ski-style HOP. The athlete is suspended IN MID-AIR — about 15-20 cm of CLEAR EMPTY FLOOR SPACE is visible directly below both feet (a small shadow on the ground far below the body confirms airborne). The body is mid-flight TRAVELING LATERALLY (sideways) to the LEFT side of the frame. BOTH FEET ARE TOGETHER and SWUNG to the LEFT side of the body (feet are about 30 cm to the left of where the head is). The HIPS AND TORSO LEAN to the LEFT, body angled sideways like a downhill skier carving a turn. Both knees slightly bent. Both arms swing to the RIGHT side of the body for counterbalance (opposite to the legs), like a skier's ski poles. Plain black athletic shorts and gray tank top. NEGATIVE: this is NOT a stationary standing pose — the body is AIRBORNE, suspended above the ground, traveling sideways through the air.
exercises.primary_muscles_en: quadriceps, gluteus maximus, and gluteus medius of both legs for lateral propulsion
exercises.secondary_muscles_en: calf muscles and core muscles for stabilization
labels_zh: the gluteus medius for lateral motion

exercises.view_angle: side view, facing right, freeze-frame photographic style
exercises.posture: Photographic freeze-frame, side view, of a forward agility hop with feet together. The athlete is suspended IN MID-AIR — about 15-20 cm of CLEAR EMPTY FLOOR SPACE is visible directly below both feet (a small shadow on the ground far below confirms airborne). BOTH FEET ARE PARALLEL and TOUCHING each other (side-by-side as one unit, like a frog hop or two-foot bunny hop). Both knees slightly bent in mid-flight. The body is mid-flight TRAVELING FORWARD (toward the right of the frame). Arms held at chest height bent at the elbows close to the body for balance. Torso upright with slight forward lean indicating forward motion. Plain black athletic shorts and gray tank top. NEGATIVE: this is NOT a running stride with feet apart in front-back position; this is NOT a single-leg hop — both feet are TOGETHER as one unit, and both feet are CURRENTLY OFF THE GROUND.
exercises.primary_muscles_en: calf muscles and quadriceps and gluteus maximus for quick reactive jumps
exercises.secondary_muscles_en: core muscles and hamstring group

exercises.view_angle: side view, facing right, freeze-frame photographic style
exercises.posture: Photographic freeze-frame at the SIGNATURE 'CYCLING-DOWN' phase of a C-skip drill (the bottom-back arc of the cycling motion). Side view facing right. The right foot is on the ball-of-foot in ground contact. The LEFT LEG is the active drill leg in mid-cycle: the left thigh has descended from horizontal back down toward the standing position (now angled about 30-45 degrees from vertical, going down), AND the left knee is BENT so the left lower leg (shin) curls BACKWARD with the left FOOT TUCKED BEHIND-AND-BELOW THE KNEE (like the bottom-back portion of a bicycle pedal stroke, tracing a C-shaped arc). The visual signature: an athlete's leg making a circular paddling motion at the BOTTOM of the cycle. Arms in running-style opposition. Plain black athletic shorts and gray tank top. NEGATIVE: NOT a standing running stride; NOT thigh held high horizontal (that is A-skip); NOT shin extended forward (that is B-skip). The defining feature is the leg mid-DESCENT with shin curled back.
exercises.primary_muscles_en: hip flexors and quadriceps and hamstring of the cycling leg, plus calf muscles of the supporting leg
exercises.secondary_muscles_en: gluteus maximus and core muscles

exercises.posture: Athletic person in a dynamic lunge stretch position: right leg in a deep lunge front position with right foot flat and right knee bent at 90 degrees; left leg extended fully back with knee close to but not touching the ground, ball of left foot on the ground; both hands placed on the floor inside the right foot for support, hips pushed forward and down to deepen the hip flexor stretch on the rear (left) leg; torso upright through the spine, looking forward; plain black athletic shorts and gray tank top
exercises.primary_muscles_en: hip flexors (psoas, rectus femoris) of the rear leg under stretch, and gluteus maximus of the front leg
exercises.secondary_muscles_en: quadriceps of the rear leg and adductor muscles
labels_zh: the hip flexors of the rear leg under stretch
the gluteus maximus of the front leg

exercises.view_angle: side view, facing right, freeze-frame photographic style
exercises.posture: Photographic freeze-frame of a pogo-style stiff-leg jump, mid-air. The athlete is suspended ABOVE THE GROUND with about 20 cm of CLEAR EMPTY FLOOR SPACE visible directly below both feet (you can see the floor and a small shadow far below). BOTH KNEES ARE LOCKED COMPLETELY STRAIGHT (zero bend, locked extension, RIGID — like vertical stiff sticks). Both legs parallel and held side-by-side. Ankles in plantarflexion (toes pointed DOWN at the floor like a ballerina). The whole body is rigid and vertical, like a pogo stick rod. Arms held at sides with slight elbow bend for balance. Torso completely upright (vertical). Plain black athletic shorts and gray tank top. NEGATIVE: this is NOT running, NOT jogging, NOT a normal jump with bent knees — knees DO NOT BEND AT ALL, only the ankles work. The body looks like a stiff stick jumping straight up.
exercises.primary_muscles_en: calf muscles (gastrocnemius and soleus) for elastic recoil and ankle stiffness
exercises.secondary_muscles_en: quadriceps and core muscles for body rigidity
labels_zh: the calf muscles for elastic recoil

exercises.view_angle: side view from ground level, the lower body and feet fill the bottom half of the frame
exercises.posture: Athletic person balanced on the TIPTOES of the RIGHT foot only, like a ballerina en pointe on a single foot. Only the BALL OF THE RIGHT TOES touches the floor; the RIGHT HEEL is lifted HIGH IN THE AIR with a visible gap of about 8-10 cm between heel and floor. The right leg is straight, supporting all body weight. The LEFT LEG is lifted entirely OFF THE GROUND, with the left knee bent at 90 degrees and the left foot tucked behind the right ankle (left foot suspended in air, NEVER touching anything). Left hand placed lightly on a wall to the left for balance. Torso upright. Plain black athletic shorts and gray tank top. EMPHASIS: the figure is balanced on a SINGLE TIPTOE (right toes only), with the right heel dramatically OFF THE FLOOR and the entire left foot in the air.
exercises.primary_muscles_en: calf muscles (gastrocnemius and soleus) of the supporting (right) leg
exercises.secondary_muscles_en: tibialis posterior and intrinsic foot muscles for stabilization

exercises.posture: Athletic person at the top of an outward lateral leg swing: right leg supporting with foot flat on the ground; left leg swung outward to the left side at about 45 degrees from the vertical, knee almost fully extended, foot relaxed; both hands placed on a wall in front of the body at shoulder height for balance, arms straight; torso upright, hips facing forward; controlled dynamic mobility movement; plain black athletic shorts and gray tank top
exercises.primary_muscles_en: gluteus medius and adductor muscles (eccentric stretch)
exercises.secondary_muscles_en: core muscles and gluteus maximus of the supporting hip
labels_zh: the core and supporting glute area

exercises.posture: Athletic person at the bottom of a lateral lunge: feet wide apart with weight shifted entirely onto the right leg, right knee bent at 90 degrees with right thigh parallel to the floor and right foot flat on the ground; left leg fully extended straight to the side with foot flat on the ground; this is at the moment of the lunge depth, similar to T1217 lateral squat but with more dynamic step-out emphasis; hands clasped at chest height; torso upright with slight forward lean; plain black athletic shorts and gray tank top
exercises.primary_muscles_en: quadriceps and gluteus maximus of the bent (right) leg, with adductor stretch on the extended (left) leg
exercises.secondary_muscles_en: gluteus medius and hamstring group
labels_zh: the quadriceps of the bent leg

exercises.posture: Athletic person performing a butt-kick running drill at the heel-up phase: right leg supporting on the ball of the foot in mid-stride contact; left knee bent so that the left heel kicks up to touch the left buttock (left foot pulled up to the glute), the left thigh remains roughly vertical (perpendicular to the floor); arms in running-style opposition; torso upright with slight forward lean; plain black athletic shorts and gray tank top
exercises.primary_muscles_en: hamstring group of the kicking (left) leg (heel-to-glute action)
exercises.secondary_muscles_en: calf muscles of the supporting leg and gluteus maximus
labels_zh: the hamstring group of the kicking leg
the calf muscles of the supporting leg

exercises.posture: Athletic person holding a forearm plank: forearms flat on the ground with elbows directly under the shoulders, forearms parallel to the body, hands relaxed; toes on the ground, feet hip-width apart; body in a perfectly straight line from heels to head with no sag at the hips and no piking up; hips at the same height as shoulders; head in line with the spine, neutral neck, eyes looking down; glutes engaged, core braced; plain black athletic shorts and gray tank top
exercises.primary_muscles_en: rectus abdominis (front of abdomen) and transverse abdominis (deep core)
exercises.secondary_muscles_en: gluteus maximus, quadriceps, and shoulder stabilizers (serratus anterior)

exercises.view_angle: front-three-quarter view from the open side
exercises.posture: Athletic person holding a left-side plank: lying on the left side with the left forearm flat on the ground (left elbow directly under the left shoulder, forearm perpendicular to the body); legs fully stacked with feet together, the left edge of the left foot on the ground; hips lifted so that the body forms one straight line from the left ankle through the hips to the head; the right arm extended straight up toward the ceiling with palm facing forward; head in line with spine; plain black athletic shorts and gray tank top
exercises.primary_muscles_en: obliques (especially the bottom side facing the ground) and quadratus lumborum
exercises.secondary_muscles_en: gluteus medius of the bottom hip and shoulder stabilizers (serratus anterior)
labels_zh: the gluteus medius of the supporting hip
the quadratus lumborum and shoulder stabilizers

exercises.view_angle: side profile view of the lateral edge of the body, the body horizontal and supported on one forearm only
exercises.posture: Athletic person holding a side plank on the LEFT side with the top leg lifted: the body is LYING ON ITS LEFT SIDE (lateral side, NOT supine, NOT prone). Only the LEFT FOREARM is on the ground (left elbow directly under left shoulder, left forearm perpendicular to the body), and ONLY the OUTER EDGE of the LEFT FOOT touches the ground supporting the body — these are the only two contact points. The body is held in one perfectly straight rigid line from the left foot through the hips to the head, parallel to the ground. Hips are lifted off the ground (not sagging). The RIGHT (top) leg is lifted upward to about 30-45 degrees above horizontal, knee fully extended. The right arm is fully extended STRAIGHT UP toward the ceiling, perpendicular to the body line. Plain black athletic shorts and gray tank top. CRITICAL: this is a SIDE PLANK position — body sideways with one forearm support — NOT a four-limb extended X-pose, NOT a supine pose, NOT a face-down pose.
exercises.primary_muscles_en: obliques and gluteus medius of both hips (top hip for abduction, bottom hip for stability)
exercises.secondary_muscles_en: quadratus lumborum and shoulder stabilizers
labels_zh: the gluteus medius of both hips

exercises.view_angle: three-quarter side view, body lying on the back, viewed from the right side at slight angle from above
exercises.posture: Athletic person mid-rep of a dead bug exercise. Lying SUPINE on the floor, lower back pressed flat to the ground. The body shows DIAGONAL OPPOSITE limb action: ONE side extends, the OTHER stays tucked. Specifically: the RIGHT ARM is reaching back overhead almost touching the floor behind the head (arm fully extended). The LEFT LEG is fully extended straight out forward, parallel to the floor, just a few centimeters above the ground (toes pointing forward). At the same time the LEFT ARM points straight up toward the ceiling (vertical arm), and the RIGHT LEG is held with knee tucked toward the chest (right thigh vertical/perpendicular to the floor with shin parallel to the floor like a tabletop). Show a CLEAR VISUAL DIAGONAL between the extended-right-arm-back-overhead and the extended-left-leg-forward; meanwhile the tucked left arm points up and the tucked right knee points up. Plain black athletic shorts and gray tank top.
exercises.primary_muscles_en: deep core (transverse abdominis and rectus abdominis) for anti-extension
exercises.secondary_muscles_en: hip flexors of the bent leg and shoulder stabilizers
labels_zh: the hip flexors and shoulder stabilizers

exercises.posture: Athletic person at the top of a bird-dog hold: in a quadruped position with hands flat on the ground directly under the shoulders and knees on the ground directly under the hips; the right arm extended forward parallel to the floor with the hand at shoulder height; the left leg extended straight backward parallel to the floor with the foot at hip height, so that right arm, torso, and left leg form one straight horizontal line; spine neutral, hips and shoulders level (no rotation), core braced; plain black athletic shorts and gray tank top
exercises.primary_muscles_en: erector spinae (back muscles along the spine) and gluteus maximus of the extended (left) leg
exercises.secondary_muscles_en: deep core (transverse abdominis) and shoulder stabilizers (rotator cuff and serratus anterior) of the extended arm
labels_zh: the gluteus maximus of the extended leg
the core and rotator cuff stabilizers

exercises.posture: Athletic person at the top of a sit-up: starting from supine position, now sitting up so that the torso is lifted off the ground at about 60-70 degrees from horizontal; both knees bent at 90 degrees with feet flat on the ground; hands placed lightly behind the head with elbows out to the sides (not pulling on the neck); chin tucked slightly, neck neutral; full contraction of the abdominal wall; plain black athletic shorts and gray tank top
exercises.primary_muscles_en: rectus abdominis (front of abdomen, full contraction)
exercises.secondary_muscles_en: hip flexors (psoas, rectus femoris) and obliques

exercises.posture: Athletic person at the top of a crunch (smaller range than sit-up): lying supine with both knees bent at 90 degrees and feet flat on the ground; only the upper back and shoulders lifted off the ground (about 30-40 degrees), with the lower back remaining in contact with the ground; hands placed lightly behind the head with elbows out, chin tucked; focused contraction of the upper rectus abdominis; plain black athletic shorts and gray tank top
exercises.primary_muscles_en: rectus abdominis (especially the upper portion)
exercises.secondary_muscles_en: obliques and transverse abdominis

exercises.view_angle: side view from low angle on the right side of the body, body lying on the floor
exercises.posture: Athletic person LYING ON THEIR BACK ON A YOGA MAT ON THE FLOOR (SUPINE position) — the LOWER BACK and HIPS remain pressed onto the mat. Only the upper torso (head and shoulders) is slightly lifted off the mat by the abdominal contraction. Both hands lightly behind the head with elbows out. The RIGHT KNEE is pulled HIGH toward the chest (right hip and knee deeply flexed). The LEFT LEG is FULLY EXTENDED forward parallel to and just above the floor (left foot hovering near floor level). The torso is rotated so the LEFT ELBOW reaches diagonally toward the RIGHT KNEE — the left elbow and right knee meet in the middle of the body. Plain black athletic shorts and gray tank top. CRITICAL CONSTRAINTS: this person is LYING DOWN, NOT seated, NOT sitting up at 45 degrees, NOT in any kind of seated V-position. The body is HORIZONTAL on the floor with only a small abdominal crunch lifting the head/shoulders. Show the floor/mat clearly underneath.
exercises.primary_muscles_en: obliques (especially the side rotating across) and rectus abdominis
exercises.secondary_muscles_en: hip flexors of the drawn-up knee and lower abs

exercises.posture: Athletic person mid-rep of a plank shoulder tap: holding a high plank position (hands on the ground under shoulders with arms straight, toes on the ground, body in one straight line); currently lifting the right hand off the ground to tap the left shoulder while balancing the body on the left hand and both feet (the supporting hand is wider for stability); hips remain level and stable (no rotation); plain black athletic shorts and gray tank top
exercises.primary_muscles_en: rectus abdominis and obliques (anti-rotation core work) and serratus anterior of the supporting arm
exercises.secondary_muscles_en: shoulder stabilizers and gluteus maximus and quadriceps
labels_zh: the serratus anterior of the supporting arm

exercises.view_angle: front-three-quarter view from the open (rotated-toward) side
exercises.posture: Athletic person at the top phase of a plank rotation — currently in a SIDE-PLANK-LIKE position after rotating 90 degrees from a high plank. The RIGHT HAND remains on the ground supporting the entire body weight, right arm fully extended. The LEFT ARM is FULLY EXTENDED STRAIGHT UP toward the ceiling, perpendicular to the body, fingers pointing up. The body is rotated 90 degrees so the left side of the torso faces the ceiling. The legs are STACKED on top of each other (left foot on top of right foot), only the right foot's outer edge contacts the ground. The body forms one straight line from feet to head. Head looks up toward the raised left hand. Plain black athletic shorts and gray tank top. CRITICAL: ONE arm is on the ground, the OTHER arm is extended straight up toward the ceiling — NOT both hands on the ground.
exercises.primary_muscles_en: obliques (rotational core) and rectus abdominis
exercises.secondary_muscles_en: shoulder stabilizers and gluteus medius
labels_zh: the shoulder and gluteus medius stabilizers

exercises.posture: Athletic person at the bottom of a standard push-up: hands on the ground slightly wider than shoulder-width with arms bent so that elbows are at about 45 degrees from the torso (not flared out); chest hovering just above the ground; toes on the ground, feet hip-width apart; body in one straight line from heels to head with no sag and no piking; head in line with spine; plain black athletic shorts and gray tank top
exercises.primary_muscles_en: pectoralis major (chest) and triceps brachii (back of arms)
exercises.secondary_muscles_en: anterior deltoid (front shoulder) and serratus anterior and core muscles
labels_zh: the anterior deltoid and core area

exercises.posture: Athletic person at the bottom of an incline push-up (hands elevated on a knee-height bench/box): hands on top of a bench shoulder-width apart; toes on the ground further away from the bench so body is at a roughly 30-45 degree angle to the floor; arms bent so chest approaches the bench; body in one straight line from heels to head; this is an easier scaled variant of push-up emphasizing the lower chest; plain black athletic shorts and gray tank top
exercises.primary_muscles_en: pectoralis major (especially the lower fibers) and triceps
exercises.secondary_muscles_en: anterior deltoid and core muscles
labels_zh: the anterior deltoid and core area

exercises.posture: Athletic person at the bottom of a decline push-up (feet elevated on a knee-height bench/box): hands on the ground shoulder-width apart; feet on top of a bench, the body angled so that the head and torso are lower than the feet (about 30-45 degrees decline); arms bent so chest approaches the floor; body in one straight line from feet to head; this is a harder variant emphasizing the upper chest and shoulders; plain black athletic shorts and gray tank top
exercises.primary_muscles_en: pectoralis major (especially the upper fibers) and anterior deltoid
exercises.secondary_muscles_en: triceps and core muscles

exercises.posture: Athletic person at the bottom of a pike push-up: hands on the ground shoulder-width apart, feet on the ground close to the hands so that the hips are pushed up high creating an upside-down V shape (downward dog position); from this position the arms bend to lower the head down between the hands toward the ground; this targets the shoulders heavily; legs straight, hips high; plain black athletic shorts and gray tank top
exercises.primary_muscles_en: deltoids (all three heads) and triceps
exercises.secondary_muscles_en: upper chest (clavicular pectoralis) and trapezius
labels_zh: 胸大肌上束 / 斜方肌

exercises.view_angle: view from above and slightly behind
exercises.posture: Athletic person doing a prone I (Y-T-W-I series): lying face-down on the floor on a mat, legs extended together; both arms fully extended overhead in a straight line with the body (forming the letter I from above), thumbs pointing toward the ceiling, palms facing each other; arms lifted off the ground a few inches by squeezing the upper back; chin slightly tucked, forehead just off the ground; this works the lower trapezius and shoulder stabilizers; plain black athletic shorts and gray tank top
exercises.primary_muscles_en: lower trapezius and rhomboids (mid-back retraction)
exercises.secondary_muscles_en: rear deltoid and erector spinae
labels_zh: 三角肌后束 / 竖脊肌
the rear deltoid and erector spinae

exercises.view_angle: bird's-eye view from directly above looking down at the floor
exercises.posture: Athletic person LYING FACE-DOWN ON A YOGA MAT ON THE FLOOR (PRONE position) — the chest, abdomen, hips, and the front of the thighs are all pressed flat into the mat. The forehead rests on the mat, head down. Legs are extended straight together. Both arms are extended ABOVE THE HEAD and angled outward at about 45 degrees from the body axis, so the body+arms form the LETTER Y as seen from above (legs together as the stem, arms as the upper branches). Thumbs pointing toward the ceiling, palms facing each other. The arms are LIFTED A FEW CENTIMETERS off the mat by squeezing the lower trapezius. Plain black athletic shorts and gray tank top. CRITICAL CONSTRAINT: this person is LYING DOWN ON THE FLOOR FACE-DOWN. The person is NOT STANDING. The person is NOT UPRIGHT. The body is HORIZONTAL ON THE GROUND. Show the full body lying flat from a top-down perspective.
exercises.primary_muscles_en: lower trapezius and rear deltoid
exercises.secondary_muscles_en: rhomboids and erector spinae

exercises.view_angle: bird's-eye view from directly above looking down at the floor
exercises.posture: Athletic person LYING FACE-DOWN ON A YOGA MAT ON THE FLOOR (PRONE position) — chest, abdomen, hips, and front of thighs pressed flat into the mat. Forehead resting on the mat, head down. Legs extended straight together. Both arms extended OUT TO THE SIDES at exactly 90 degrees from the body axis, so the body+arms form the LETTER T as seen from above (legs together as the stem, arms as the horizontal cross). Thumbs pointing toward the ceiling, palms facing the floor. The arms are LIFTED A FEW CENTIMETERS off the mat by retracting the shoulder blades together. Plain black athletic shorts and gray tank top. CRITICAL CONSTRAINT: this person is LYING DOWN ON THE FLOOR FACE-DOWN. The person is NOT STANDING. The person is NOT UPRIGHT. The body is HORIZONTAL ON THE GROUND. Show the full body lying flat from a top-down perspective.
exercises.primary_muscles_en: middle trapezius and rhomboids (scapular retraction)
exercises.secondary_muscles_en: rear deltoid and erector spinae
labels_zh: 三角肌后束 / 竖脊肌
the rear deltoid and erector spinae

exercises.view_angle: bird's-eye view from directly above looking down at the floor
exercises.posture: Athletic person LYING FACE-DOWN ON A YOGA MAT ON THE FLOOR (PRONE position) — chest, abdomen, hips, and front of thighs pressed flat into the mat. Forehead resting on the mat, head down. Legs extended straight together. Both arms bent at the elbows: shoulders abducted out to the sides at about 90 degrees from the body axis, elbows bent so the forearms come back toward the head with hands near the ears, thumbs pointing toward the ceiling. From a top-down view this forms the LETTER W (the head and arms together making a W shape). The arms are LIFTED A FEW CENTIMETERS off the mat by retracting and depressing the shoulder blades. Plain black athletic shorts and gray tank top. CRITICAL CONSTRAINT: this person is LYING DOWN ON THE FLOOR FACE-DOWN. The person is NOT STANDING. The person is NOT UPRIGHT. The body is HORIZONTAL ON THE GROUND. Show the full body lying flat from a top-down perspective.
exercises.primary_muscles_en: lower trapezius and external rotators of the shoulders (infraspinatus, teres minor)
exercises.secondary_muscles_en: rhomboids and rear deltoid
labels_zh: the external rotators of the shoulders
菱形肌 / 三角肌后束

exercises.posture: Athletic person performing a thoracic spine rotation in quadruped position: starting on hands and knees (hands under shoulders, knees under hips); right hand placed lightly behind the head with elbow out to the side; rotating the upper back so that the right elbow points up toward the ceiling, opening the chest to the right side; the left arm remains supporting on the ground; lower back and hips remain stable (rotation is from the thoracic spine only, not the lumbar); plain black athletic shorts and gray tank top
exercises.primary_muscles_en: thoracic spine rotators and obliques
exercises.secondary_muscles_en: rhomboids and serratus anterior of the supporting arm

exercises.posture: Athletic person at the deepest phase of the world's greatest stretch (low lunge with thoracic rotation): right foot in a deep lunge front position with right knee bent at 90 degrees, foot flat; left leg extended back with knee just above the ground, ball of left foot on the ground; left hand placed on the ground inside the right foot for support; right arm reaching straight up toward the ceiling with the chest opened and rotated to the right; head following the right hand; full-body dynamic stretch combining hip flexor stretch, glute stretch, and thoracic rotation; plain black athletic shorts and gray tank top
exercises.primary_muscles_en: hip flexors of the rear leg under stretch and gluteus maximus of the front leg, and thoracic rotators
exercises.secondary_muscles_en: obliques and adductors and shoulder stabilizers
labels_zh: the hip flexors of the rear leg
the gluteus maximus of the front leg
胸椎旋转肌 / 腹斜肌

exercises.view_angle: side view from the open side
exercises.posture: Athletic person holding a Copenhagen side plank (long-lever variant with bent bottom leg) on the LEFT side: lying on the LEFT side. Left forearm flat on the ground (left elbow directly under left shoulder, left forearm perpendicular to the body line). The TOP (RIGHT) LEG is FULLY EXTENDED STRAIGHT forward with the right ANKLE / LOWER SHIN resting on top of a knee-height bench/box (the bench supports the body weight at the foot end of the top leg). The BOTTOM (LEFT) LEG is BENT at the knee at about 90 degrees, with the left foot tucked back behind the buttocks and the left knee pointing forward (the bent left leg is held IN THE AIR, OFF THE GROUND, not touching anything). Hips lifted high off the ground so the body forms a roughly straight line from the LEFT shoulder through the hips to the right foot supported on the bench. Right arm extended straight up toward the ceiling. Plain black athletic shorts and gray tank top. CRITICAL: TOP (RIGHT) leg is STRAIGHT and rests on the bench at the foot/ankle; BOTTOM (LEFT) leg is BENT at the knee and held in the air. The bottom leg is NOT straight, the top leg is NOT bent.
exercises.primary_muscles_en: adductor muscles of the bottom (left) leg (inner thigh)
exercises.secondary_muscles_en: obliques and gluteus medius and shoulder stabilizers
labels_zh: the gluteus medius and shoulder stabilizers

exercises.view_angle: three-quarter view from the front and slightly above
exercises.posture: Athletic person in a pigeon pose stretch: starting from a quadruped position, the right leg has been brought forward and placed under the body so that the right knee is bent and pointing forward, the right shin lies horizontally across the body (right foot near the left hip, right shin roughly parallel to the front edge of the mat). The left leg is fully extended straight back, with the top of the left foot resting flat on the ground. The torso leans gently forward over the front (right) leg, with both forearms or hands flat on the ground in front of the body for support. Hips squared toward the front, deep stretch in the right glute and outer hip. Plain black athletic shorts and gray tank top.
exercises.primary_muscles_en: gluteus maximus and piriformis of the front (right) hip
exercises.secondary_muscles_en: hip flexors of the rear (left) leg and gluteus medius of the front hip
labels_zh: the gluteus maximus of the front hip
the gluteus medius and hip flexor area

exercises.posture: Athletic person in a kneeling lunge hip flexor stretch position: front leg (right) bent with foot flat on the ground and knee at 90 degrees, knee directly over ankle. Rear leg (left) kneeling with the knee on the ground (on a soft mat) and the top of the foot pointing back. Hips pushed forward to feel the stretch in the front of the rear (left) hip and thigh. Torso completely upright (vertical), or both arms raised straight overhead with hands together to deepen the hip flexor stretch. Plain black athletic shorts and gray tank top. CRITICAL: the rear LEFT KNEE is on the ground, the front RIGHT KNEE is over the right ankle at 90 degrees.
exercises.primary_muscles_en: hip flexors (psoas major and rectus femoris) of the rear (left) leg under stretch
exercises.secondary_muscles_en: quadriceps of the rear leg and gluteus maximus of the front leg
labels_zh: the iliopsoas (hip flexor) of the rear leg
the rectus femoris of the rear leg
the gluteus maximus of the front leg

exercises.posture: Athletic person in a quadruped position performing the COW phase of the cat-cow stretch: hands flat on the ground directly under shoulders (arms straight, fingers spread), knees on the ground directly under hips. The back is gently ARCHED downward (lumbar extension) — belly drops toward the floor, chest lifts up and forward, the head tilts up to look slightly forward and up. This is the OPEN/COW phase. Plain black athletic shorts and gray tank top.
exercises.primary_muscles_en: erector spinae and rectus abdominis (eccentric stretch through spinal flexion-extension cycle)
exercises.secondary_muscles_en: thoracic spine mobilizers and pelvic floor muscles
labels_zh: the rectus abdominis (under stretch in cow phase)

exercises.view_angle: front view, focused on lower leg and foot
exercises.posture: Athletic person seated on the floor with the right leg extended straight forward and the left leg bent comfortably to the side. A loop resistance band is attached to a fixed anchor on the FAR-RIGHT side at floor level (LATERAL to the right foot, beyond the pinky-toe side), with the loop wrapped around the ball of the right foot. The right foot is rotated INWARD (ankle inversion): the SOLE of the right foot is visibly TURNED to face the LEFT KNEE (toward the body's midline). The OUTER (PINKY-TOE) edge of the right foot is TILTED HIGH UP, the INNER (BIG-TOE) edge is rolled DOWN toward the floor. The band stretches OUTWARD from the foot to the right anchor, while the foot is held INWARD against the band's pull. View: front-three-quarter angle so both the band path AND the foot rotation are clearly visible. Plain black athletic shorts and gray tank top.
exercises.primary_muscles_en: tibialis posterior (inner side of the lower leg/ankle)
exercises.secondary_muscles_en: tibialis anterior and small intrinsic foot muscles

exercises.view_angle: front view, focused on lower leg and foot
exercises.posture: Athletic person seated on the floor with the right leg extended STRAIGHT FORWARD, the right HEEL resting on the floor and the toes pointing slightly upward. The left leg is bent with the foot tucked back behind the buttocks, completely OUT OF THE WAY. A blue elastic loop resistance band is wrapped in a U-SHAPE around the FOREFOOT of the right foot (around the ball / metatarsals area). Both ENDS of the U-shaped band exit the foot on the INNER (BIG-TOE / MEDIAL) side of the foot — they run together as a parallel pair from the medial side of the right forefoot, downward and to the LEFT, ending at a small fixed floor anchor on the LEFT side of the body (medial to the right foot). The two band strands form a clearly visible TUG of resistance pulling the forefoot toward the LEFT/MEDIAL side. The right ankle is shown ROTATED OUTWARD against this medial pull (ankle eversion): the SOLE of the right foot tilts so it visibly faces the FAR RIGHT, away from the body's midline. The BIG-TOE edge of the foot is HIGH, the PINKY-TOE edge is LOW. View: front-three-quarter so both the band wrap on the forefoot AND the foot rotation are clearly visible. Plain black athletic shorts and gray tank top. CRITICAL: the resistance band must clearly WRAP AROUND the OUTSIDE of the forefoot like a strap around a package — it must NOT emerge from inside the foot, must NOT pass through any body part, and the foot must be solidly visible underneath the wrap.
exercises.primary_muscles_en: peroneal muscles (peroneus longus and brevis, outer side of the lower leg)
exercises.secondary_muscles_en: tibialis anterior and small intrinsic foot muscles

exercises.posture: Athletic person performing a SINGLE-LEG wall sit on the right leg: back, shoulders, and head pressed flat against a vertical wall. Right foot flat on the ground, positioned about a thigh-length out from the wall. Right knee bent at exactly 90 degrees, right thigh parallel to the floor, right shin vertical — supporting all body weight. The LEFT LEG is lifted entirely OFF THE GROUND with the left thigh lifted forward and held parallel to the floor at the same height as the right thigh, left knee bent so the left shin angles forward. Arms hanging at sides or crossed at chest. Plain black athletic shorts and gray tank top. CRITICAL: only the RIGHT FOOT is on the ground, the left foot is in the air; this is the SINGLE-LEG variant of wall sit.
exercises.primary_muscles_en: quadriceps of the supporting (right) leg under heavy isometric load
exercises.secondary_muscles_en: gluteus maximus of the supporting leg, hip flexors of the lifted leg, and core
labels_zh: the quadriceps of the supporting leg
the gluteus maximus of the supporting leg
the hip flexors of the lifted leg and the core

exercises.view_angle: side view from the right, focused on the lower leg and foot
exercises.posture: Athletic person seated on a chair, right leg extended forward with the right heel resting on the floor (knee almost fully extended, slight bend). The right foot is FULLY DORSIFLEXED — the toes and forefoot are pulled UP toward the shin as far as possible, creating a sharp angle at the ankle. The contracted tibialis anterior muscle bulges visibly along the front-outer side of the right shin (lateral aspect of the tibia). Left leg is bent and resting normally. Plain black athletic shorts and gray tank top.
exercises.primary_muscles_en: tibialis anterior (front-outer side of the shin)
exercises.secondary_muscles_en: extensor digitorum longus (toe extensors)

exercises.posture: Athletic person standing on a BOSU half-ball training device with the dome side facing UP (the flat platform is on the floor, the rubber dome is what the foot stands on). Only the RIGHT FOOT is balanced on the center of the dome. The LEFT LEG is lifted off the ground with the left knee bent to 90 degrees in front of the body (left thigh approximately parallel to the floor). Both arms are extended out to the sides at shoulder height for balance. Right knee is slightly bent for shock absorption. Torso upright with neutral spine. Plain black athletic shorts and gray tank top.
exercises.primary_muscles_en: ankle stabilizers of the supporting leg (peroneals, tibialis posterior, intrinsic foot muscles)
exercises.secondary_muscles_en: quadriceps and gluteus medius of the supporting leg, and core stabilizers

exercises.posture: Athletic person holding a forearm plank with one leg lifted: forearms on the ground (elbows under shoulders), body in a straight line from heels to head. The LEFT LEG is on the ground with the left toes supporting the body. The RIGHT LEG is lifted straight up off the ground, knee fully extended, with the right foot hovering about 15-20 cm above the floor. Hips remain level (no rotation). Glutes engaged. Plain black athletic shorts and gray tank top.
exercises.primary_muscles_en: rectus abdominis and obliques (anti-extension and anti-rotation core work) and gluteus maximus of the lifted leg
exercises.secondary_muscles_en: shoulder stabilizers and quadriceps
labels_zh: the gluteus maximus of the lifted leg
肩稳定肌 / 股四头肌

exercises.posture: Athletic person performing a banded lateral walk: standing in a quarter-squat position with knees slightly bent (about 30 degrees of knee flexion) and hips pushed back. A loop resistance band is wrapped around both ankles, kept taut. The person is mid-step, taking a wide lateral step to the right with the right foot, while the left leg remains slightly bent and stable. The band creates outward tension that the gluteus medius works against. Torso upright. Hands at hips. Plain black athletic shorts and gray tank top.
exercises.primary_muscles_en: gluteus medius (especially of the moving and stabilizing legs)
exercises.secondary_muscles_en: gluteus maximus and quadriceps and ankle stabilizers
labels_zh: 股四头肌 / 踝稳定肌

exercises.view_angle: front-three-quarter view from above and slightly to the side
exercises.posture: Athletic person seated on the floor in a 90/90 hip position: the right leg is in front with the right hip externally rotated, right knee bent at 90 degrees with the right thigh perpendicular to the body (knee pointing out to the right side) and the right shin lying horizontally across the body. The left leg is behind to the left side with the left hip internally rotated, left knee bent at 90 degrees with the left thigh angled to the left and the left shin lying horizontally pointing backward (knee pointing forward, foot pointing back). Torso upright with hands either on the floor at the sides or on the front knee. Plain black athletic shorts and gray tank top.
exercises.primary_muscles_en: hip rotators (deep external and internal rotators) of both hips, with stretch on the gluteal complex
exercises.secondary_muscles_en: adductors and hip flexors and gluteus medius
labels_zh: the adductor and hip flexor area
